codeReview.summary: The feature introduces a dynamic `usePillars()` source and removes hardcoded personal references from `src/`, but multiple pillar flows still fall back to hardcoded default pillar values and one cache design causes cross-user pillar leakage risk.

issues.description: `usePillars` caches pillar data in a single module-level `_cache` without user scoping. Because the hook returns cached data before checking `getCurrentUser()` (`line 106`), switching accounts in the same client session can show the previous user's custom pillars.

Selected pillar initializes from `pillarList[0] ?? 'hot-take'` before async custom pillars load, and is never reconciled after list changes. For custom-pillar users this can leave `pillar='hot-take'`, causing generation to use hardcoded legacy prompts (`line 9`) instead of user-defined pillars.
Library `handleNewPost` still hardcodes `pillar: 'hot-take'`, so creating a post can immediately reintroduce a legacy pillar for users with custom pillar definitions.
Ideas quick-add defaults to `pillarList[0] ?? 'hot-take'` at initial render and does not re-sync when custom pillars load, so submissions can persist `hot-take` even when user-defined pillars exist.

StoryMine prompt still hardcodes legacy pillar taxonomy (`hot-take/hackathon/founder/...`), and conversion fallback still uses `'hot-take'` (`line 133`). Related hardcoded defaults also remain in `SeriesPlanner` (`content-os/src/components/generate/SeriesPlanner.tsx:75,109`) and Story Bank conversion (`content-os/src/app/(dashboard)/story-bank/page.tsx:81`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill procedure and worker behavior are out of sync for UI verification expectations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `fullstack-worker` requires agent-browser/manual UI checks for UI features (`content-os/.factory/skills/fullstack-worker/SKILL.md`, sections "Required Skills" and "Manual verification"), but this worker handoff reports command-only verification and the transcript skeleton filter for this session shows no `agent-browser` usage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mutation-boundary rule is documented but not enforced in feature work; direct client-side writes continue to be introduced in modified files.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS and architecture both state mutations should go through API routes (`/Users/anirudhmanjesh/.factory/missions/fbcce583-7257-4bc1-9707-8c0ee4dcc7ec/AGENTS.md` Data Access section, `content-os/.factory/library/architecture.md` Key Invariants). This feature still writes directly via client SDK in `content-os/src/components/generate/GenerateOutput.tsx:89-90` and `content-os/src/components/generate/SeriesPlanner.tsx:70-71,120-121`.

summary: Review failed. Dynamic pillar plumbing is present in many surfaces and personal-name grep checks are clean, but blocking hardcoded pillar defaults remain in Generate/Library/Ideas flows and the global pillar cache is not user-scoped.
